franchisor_address: 6191 North State Highway 161, Suite 400, Irving, TX 75038-2251

celebrity_figure.name: Gordon Ramsay
celebrity_figure.role: Public figure; 49% indirect owner of GRNA Holdings
celebrity_figure.compensation: No direct compensation for endorsement/name use; receives indirect financial benefit from fees
celebrity_figure.involvement: Not involved in day-to-day management; may make personal promotional appearances

concept.description: Fast-casual restaurant featuring classic English street food, including British fish and chips, chicken and shrimp, milkshakes, beer and wine service
venue_types: non-traditional venues (food courts, military bases, hospitals, campuses, hotels, stadiums, arenas, casinos, airports)

system_size.notes: Franchised outlets are licensees under affiliate IPH2, not actual franchise agreements. No actual franchisees as of issuance date.

royalty.escalation: May increase to 8% for Menu Item Price Policy violations

brand_fund.cap: Combined with local marketing not to exceed 4%
brand_fund.status: Not yet established as of issuance date

local_marketing.cap: Combined with brand fund not to exceed 4%

development_termination_fee.formula: Greater of: $270,000 × undeveloped units OR avg royalty × 24 × undeveloped units

liquidated_damages.formula: If <3 yrs remaining: greater of (avg royalty × months remaining) or $500,000. If ≥3 yrs and operated ≥2 yrs: avg royalty × 24. If ≥3 yrs and operated <2 yrs: system avg royalty × 24.

pre_opening_royalty.trigger: Restaurant not opened by required deadline

territory.notes: Franchisor determines Designated Area. Not exclusive — broad reserved rights for non-traditional venues, collateral products, other brands within territory, delivery from outside territory.
reserved_rights: Operate Fish & Chips or other brands outside Designated Area
Sell collateral products anywhere through any channel
Operate any non-Fish & Chips business inside Designated Area
Operate Fish & Chips in Reserved Areas (airports, hotels, casinos, etc.) inside Designated Area
Deliver/cater from outside territory into Designated Area

renewal_conditions: Good standing under FA and all other agreements
Right to remain in possession of premises
Sign then-current FA (may be materially different)

during_term.scope: No involvement with any 'Competitive Business' — (1) same/substantially similar to Fish & Chips including any restaurant where British-style fish and chips ≥25% of menu/gross sales, OR (2) any restaurant identified by/promoted with a celebrity chef

post_term.geographic: Within Designated Area OR within 3 miles of any Restaurant

dispute_resolution.litigation_forum: State or federal courts encompassing Dallas, TX

conditions: General release by franchisee and principals

training.location: Corporate office (Irving TX) and/or designated restaurant

opening_crew.size: Up to 2 culinary + 2 service team members

supplier_restrictions.rebates: May derive and retain rebates/commissions from supplier dealings; none received as of issuance

trademarks.license_chain: Gordon Ramsay → GRNA IP HoldCo LLC (exclusive license, 10-yr + auto-renew) → GRNA FC Group LLC (nonexclusive sublicense)

item19_fpr.notes: No financial performance representation made. Franchisor expressly declines to provide FPR.

financial_statements.reason_incomplete: Company formed June 2024; not in business for 3+ years; no operations as of statement date

special_risks.description: Franchise agreement and ADA require disputes resolved in Texas. May force less favorable settlement, higher costs.
Franchisor is at early stage of development with limited operating history. Riskier investment than franchise with longer track record.

key_overrides.California: Venue/arbitration/choice of law may be unenforceable; noncompete may be unenforceable; liquidated damages may be unenforceable under Civil Code 1671; max interest 10%
key_overrides.Indiana: No unilateral termination without good cause; noncompete modified per IC 23-2-2.7-1(9)
key_overrides.Maryland: FA/ADA provisions superseded by MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law; general release not required as condition of renewal
key_overrides.Minnesota: MN franchise laws supersede; noncompete limited; general release not required
key_overrides.Washington: Venue in WA or mutually agreed; initial fees deferred until training complete and open; noncompete salary thresholds per RCW 49.62

red_flags.detail: GRNA FC Group LLC formed June 2024, funded with $100K on Jan 30, 2025. No actual franchise agreements signed. No franchisee operating history.
No Item 19 disclosure. Prospective franchisees have no disclosed unit economics for a $1.5M–$2.4M investment.
Only $100,000 in total assets. Entity has not commenced operations. No income statement or cash flow.
Minimum $500,000 liquidated damages on default termination if <3 years remaining. Development Termination Fee of $270K per undeveloped unit.
Competitive Business definition includes ANY restaurant identified by or promoted in connection with a celebrity chef — extends far beyond fish and chips.
Royalty can be increased from 5% to 8% at franchisor's sole option if franchisee violates Menu Item Price Policy — gives franchisor effective price control.
$1.5M–$2.4M for a 2,000 sq ft fast-casual restaurant with no disclosed unit economics.
Franchise model is explicitly described as 'prototype based largely on affiliates' experiences' with other Gordon Ramsay brands in hotels/casinos/resorts. Untested in standalone franchise context.

red_flags.flag: Broad reserved rights for territory encroachment
red_flags.detail: Franchisor reserves right to operate in Reserved Areas (airports, hotels, casinos, stadiums, etc.) within Designated Area; can deliver/cater into territory; can operate other brands in territory.

unresolveds.question: Whether affiliate hotel/casino/resort experience translates to standalone fast-casual franchise model
Whether $1.5M–$2.4M investment is competitive for 2,000 sq ft fast-casual with no disclosed unit economics
Effective royalty burden under Menu Item Price Policy — when would 8% royalty trigger
Scope of 'celebrity chef' noncompete — practical impact on franchisee if they want to exit and operate another restaurant
